8192.built_by: Associated Ship Building Inc. (Seattle, Washington, U.S.A.)
8192.history: Decommissioned on 26 July 1946 Loaned to the United States Coast Guard as USCGC Unimak (WAVP-379) Redesignated high endurance cutter (WHEC-379) on 1 May 1966 Redesignated (WTR-379) on 28 November 1969 Decommissioned on 29 May 1975 Recommissioned as (WHEC-379) in 1977 Decommissioned on 29 April 1988 and returned to the USN for disposal Scuttled as part of an artifical reef. See also this website (offsite link).
commanders: T/Cdr. Hilfort Craft Owen, USN (31 Dec 1943 - 30 Sep 1944 (1))
T/Cdr. William Alvah Thorn, USN (30 Sep 1944 - 26 Jul 1946)

8193.built_by: Associated Ship Building Inc. (Seattle, Washington, U.S.A.)
8193.history: Decommissioned on 17 April 1946 Loaned to the United States Coast Guard and commissioned as USCGC Yakutat (WAVP-380) on 23 November 1948 Redesignated high endurance cutter (WHEC-380) on 1 May 1966 Decommissioned by the United States Coast Guard in 1970 Transferred to South Vietnam on 10 January 1971 being renamed Tran Nhat Duat (HQ-03) Taken over by the Philippines in April 1975 Sold to the Philippines on 6 April 1976 and cannibalized for spare parts
commanders: T/Cdr. George Kittrell Fraser, USN (31 Mar 1944 - 21 Jun 1945 (1))
T/Lt.Cdr. William Irvin Darnell, USN (21 Jun 1945 - 17 Apr 1946)

8194.history: Decommissioned`on 24 July 1946 Loaned to the United States Coast Guard and commissioned as USCGC Batataria (WAVP-381) on 1 August 1949 Redesignated high endurance cutter (WHEC-381) on 1 May 1966 Decommissioned in August 1969 Sold to be broken up for scrap in September 1970
commanders: T/Cdr. Garrett Steele Coleman, USN (13 Aug 1944 - 1946 (1))

8195.history: Decommissioned on 21 June 1946 Loaned to the United States Coast Guard and commissioned as USCGC Bering Strait (WAVP-382) on 14 September 1948 Redesignated high endurance cutter (WHEC-382) on 1 May 1966 Decommissioned in February 1970 Transferred to South Vietnam in January 1971 being renamed Tran Quang Khai (HQ-15) Sold to the Philippines on 5 April 1976 and commissioned in the Philippine Navy as Diego Silang (PF-9) Decommissioned by Philippine Navy in June 1985
commanders: T/Cdr. Walter Deane Innis, USN (19 Jul 1944 - late 1945 (1))

8293.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-81) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 12 February 1947 Recommissioned on 11 September 1951 Decommissioned on 25 November 1955 Sold to Italy on 1 May 1979, As of 2006 still in service with the Italian Coast Guard.

8294.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8294.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-82) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 24 January 1947 Stricken on 1 July 1963 Sold to Columbia on 14 February 1978 being renamed Pedro de Heredia

8295.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-83) on 15 May 1944 Stricken on 15 April 1976 Sold to Taiwan on 1 May 1976 being renamed Ta Tung (A-540)

8296.built_by: United Engineering Co. (San Francisco, California, U.S.A.)
8296.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-84) on 15 May 1944 Stricken on 21 April 1978 Sunk as target on 27 August 1978

8297.built_by: United Engineering Co. (San Francisco, California, U.S.A.)
8297.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-85) on 15 May 1944 Loaned to the United States Coast Guard and comissioned as USCGC Lipan (WMEC-85) on 30 September 1980 Decommissioned on 31 March 1988 and returned to the USN Stricken on 23 January 1989 Sunk as target on 22 January 1990

8298.built_by: United Engineering Co. (San Francisco, California, U.S.A.)
8298.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-86) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 1 October 1977 Sold to be broken up for scrap on 1 April 1979

8299.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-87) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 18 August 1946 Stricken on 1 September 1961

8300.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-88) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 21 December 1946 Striken on 1 September 1961

8301.history: USS Nauset (Lt. J. Orleck) was bombed and sunk off Salerno, Italy in position 40°38'N, 14°38'E
commanders: Lt. Joseph Orleck, USN (< Sep 43 - ???)

8302.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-90) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 11 July 1946 Sold to Peru on 1 May 1974 being renamed Guardian Rios Stricken on 17 May 1974 As of 2003 Guardian Rios is still in service with the Peruvian Navy

8303.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-91) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned in July 1971 Stricken on 30 October 1985 Sunk as target on 21 July 2003

8304.built_by: Commercial Iron Works, Portland, Oregon, U.S.A.)
8304.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-92) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 31 March 1975 Stricken on 1 April 1975 Sold to be broken up for scrap on 1 August 1976

8305.built_by: Commercial Iron Works, Portland, Oregon, U.S.A.)
8305.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-93) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 14 April 1950 Transferred to Chile in May 1960 being renamed Yelcho (AGS-64) Stricken on 25 June 1992

8306.built_by: Commercial Iron Works, Portland, Oregon, U.S.A.)
8306.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-94) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 11 March 1959 Stricken and transferred to Pakistan being renamed Madadgar on 25 March 1959

8307.built_by: Commercial Iron Works, Portland, Oregon, U.S.A.)
8307.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-95) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned and transferred to the United States Coast Guard, being renamed USCGC Tamaroa (WAT-166) on 29 June 1946 Stricken on 19 July 1946 Decommissioned by the United States Coast Guard on 1 February 1994

8308.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8308.history: Reclassified as fleet ocean tug (ATF-96) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 30 September 1978 Sold to Mexico on 1 September 1978 being renamed Yaqui (A-18) Stricken on 30 September 1978 As of 2003 Yaqui is still in service

8309.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8309.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-97) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 15 April 1955 Stricken on 1 September 1962 Sold to be broken up for scrap in 1975

8310.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8310.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-98) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 1 July 1971 and immediately transferred to Chile as Sargento Aledea (ATF 63). Decommissioned by the Chilean Navy on 14 August 1992. Sunk as a target. See also this website (offsite link).

8311.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8311.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-100) on 15 May 1944 Stricken and sold to Ecuador on 1 October 1977

8312.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8312.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-101) on 15 May 1944 Sold to Mexico on 1 September 1978 Stricken on 30 September 1978 As of 2003 she is still in service in the Mexican Navy as Seri

8313.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8313.history: Redesignated fleet ocean tug (ATF-102) on 15 May 1944 Decommissioned on 5 May 1948 Stricken on 1 July 1963 Sold to Colombia on 1 March 1979 being renamed Rodrigo de Bastidas As of 2003 Rodrigo de Bastidas is still in service in the Colombian Navy

8314.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8314.history: Decommissioned on 30 April 1948 Recommissioned on 3 January 1951 Decommissioned at an unknown date Sold to Mexico on 1 September 1976 Stricken on 30 September 1978 As of 2003 still in service in the Mexican Navy as Cora

8315.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8315.history: Decommissioned on 14 June 1950 Stricken on 1 July 1963 Sold to Columbia on 1 March 1979 being renamed Sebatian De Belalcazar

8316.built_by: Charleston Shipbuilding and Dry Dock Co. (Charleston, South Carolina, U.S.A.)
8316.history: Decommissioned on 30 September 1985 Stricken on 27 January 1992 Sold into commercial service on 29 December 1997

8317.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-106) on 15 May 1944 Stricken on 1 August 1978 and sold to Mexico being renamed Otomi (A-17) on the same day As of 2003 Otomi is still serving in the Mexican Navy

8318.history: Reclassified fleet ocean tug (ATF-107) on 15 May 1944
